articles.title: EU approves emergency aid after strong quake in Turkey
articles.content_text: European Union officials approved emergency aid after a strong earthquake hit Turkey. Rescue teams were deployed, Ankara said the death toll could rise, and aid logistics were coordinated with Brussels and the Turkish government.

articles.title: Europe sends emergency support as Turkey earthquake deaths rise
articles.content_text: European governments moved emergency support to Turkey after a strong earthquake. Officials described rescue operations, rising casualties, and coordination with EU civil protection mechanisms and Ankara authorities.

articles.title: European Central Bank cuts rates amid weak growth outlook
articles.content_text: The European Central Bank cut interest rates as policymakers warned about weak growth, slowing credit demand, and inflation risks across the euro area.

articles.title: Ariane rocket launch delayed after technical review
articles.content_text: Ariane launch teams delayed a scheduled rocket mission after a technical review identified a systems issue. Engineers said the flight would be rescheduled after more checks.

articles.title: Trump campaign promises broader tariffs on Chinese goods
articles.content_text: Donald Trump campaign advisers outlined broader tariffs on Chinese goods and described the policy as central to manufacturing and supply-chain strategy in a future administration.

articles.title: China warns of retaliation over proposed US tariff escalation
articles.content_text: Chinese officials warned they would retaliate if the United States escalated tariffs. The response focused on diplomatic messaging, export pressure, and trade retaliation scenarios rather than the campaign announcement itself.
